Attached please find a letter which was sent to 
Mr. Scott Carey of the real estate brokerage firm of 
Cornish and Carey. 
Mr. Carey called me and indicated 
that he wa::; the councilman about whom you and I had talked. 
He

interest in the firm. 
It was in light of this information 
that I advised him that he does not have a disclosure 
obligation at this time.

Dear Hr. Carey: 
May 10, 1976 
This letter will serve to confirw our telephone conversation 
of Friday, May 7, 1976. 
In that conversation, you indicated 
.that you presently are not a shareholder and do not possess "any 
ownership interest in the real estate brokerage firm of Cornish 
and Carey. 
You stated that you receive in excess of 10% of the 
profits of the firm but that these payments are compensation in 
exchange for services rendered to the firm. 
I indicated to you 
that under these circumstances you are not required to disclose 
the income of the business entity of Cornish and Carey pursuant 
to Government Code Section 87207(b) and that you, therefore, do 
not have to reveal the name of every person from whom the broker-
age firm received fees of which your pro.rata share would be 
more than $1,000. 
This advice was based on the definition of 
" income which states that income includes the income of any busi-
ness entity in which a person "owns, directly, indirectly or 
beneficially, a ~en percent interest or greater •••• " and the 
conclusion that under the facts presented you do not have an 
ownership interest in Cornish and Carey. 
See Government Code 
Section 82030(a). 
You also indicated~that next year you will acquire an owner-
ship interest in the firm of Cornish and Carey which will exceed 
the requisi te "10% conta ined in the definition of income. 
\\e 
discussed whether, and under what circumstances, names of clients 
of the brokerage firm would have to be disclosed, and you agreed 
to submit an opinion request concerning this issue. 
I look 
forward to receiving that request and the relevant information 
from you. 
Very trul~-y , rs,

sections.question_synthetic: Must a city councilman who receives over 10% of profits from a real estate brokerage firm but has no ownership interest in the firm disclose client names and income information from the firm under California's conflict of interest disclosure laws?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that no disclosure obligation exists under Government Code Section 87207(b) because the individual receives only compensation for services, not an ownership interest, in the real estate firm. The advice notes that future acquisition of a 10% or greater ownership interest would trigger different disclosure requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Two-letter format: covering letter from Finney to City Attorney and enclosed formal advice letter to Mr. Carey. Document clearly structured with discussion of GC §87207(b) and §82030(a). No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION headers but substantive legal analysis present. OCR quality generally good with minor formatting artifacts.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that a Palo Alto city councilman receiving over 10% of profits from Cornish and Carey real estate brokerage firm has no current disclosure obligation under state law because he has no ownership interest in the firm, only compensation for services. The matter is addressed through correspondence between Kenneth Finney (FPPC Chief of Legal Division) and both the city attorney and the councilman.
